fairness.initial_liquidity_rule: ETF A/B use equal initial reserve value per active token under each scenario.
fairness.fee_rule: ETF A/B use the same fee_bps sampled per scenario.
fairness.swap_rule: ETF A/B use the same swap ratio and swap amount per run.
notes: Cold-start CU is separated from steady-state CU.
Gate evaluation is environment-local and never mixed across layers.
Sampler auto-runs additional attempts (up to AB_MAX_ATTEMPT_MULT=4x) to hit target comparable N per scenario.

environments.name: LiteSVM
notes: Fast iteration environment; conclusions stay within LiteSVM layer.
A/B gate uses grouped comparison: PFDA-3 executes 3-token batch path while G3M executes 2-token path on the same active swap pair.

checks.detail: samples_ok=true (4 scenarios >=30 comparable runs), candidate(pfda3) p95_total_cu=46770.05 vs baseline(g3m) 39286.35 (limit <= +30%)
candidate(pfda3) p95 slots=11.00 (limit <= 30; ≈ batch window + buffer); reference success rates pfda/g3m = 74.63% / 100.00%
candidate(pfda3) critical invariant violations=0 (must be 0); reference: g3m violations=0, candidate tx-success=74.63% (informational — strict-mode oracle rejections by design)

environments.name: local-validator
notes: Run local-validator transaction-behavior benchmark separately and publish as an isolated layer.
Do not mix this layer with LiteSVM conclusions.

environments.name: devnet/mainnet-fork
notes: Run real routing / fork validation separately and publish as an isolated layer.
Do not mix this layer with LiteSVM or local-validator conclusions.
